steps.title: Spot the two related functions
steps.content: The method serves two purposes: removing byproducts from click chemistry and protecting healthy cells. The blank must properly connect these.

steps.title: Apply the standard purpose structure
steps.content:
The infinitive to protect (A) is the standard English form to express a method's intended outcome—”method for X to achieve Y.” While and protecting (C) could theoretically join two coordinate purposes, it creates mixed parallel structure (“the removal of...” with “protecting...”) that's less standard. Protected (B) is ungrammatical; protecting (D) becomes an ambiguous modifier.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
